{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Levitsky Attack| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |pd| | |bl| |= | | | |pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl| |ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} =Levitsky Attack or Queen's Bishop Attack= ===2. Bg5=== Bg5 is an immediate attack by white, in hopes of slowing black's development via pinning the pawn on e7. This position can be negated by black developing Nf6, or a pawn move f6. usually, the bishop cannot remain in its position for long, necessitating a loss of tempo. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. d4 d5 2. Bg5''' Black's main options are:
2 3 4
